---
editor: 
  markdown: 
    wrap: 72
---

# **Histology / Tissues**

images/\_page_75_Picture_1.jpeg

**Figure 4.1** Slide Underneath a Microscope.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **Objectives**

During this lab you will use the microscope and diagrams to review the
four tissue types as well as familiarize yourself with their location
and function in the body.

Upon completion of this lab session, you should be able to:

-   Name a tissue as epithelial, connective, muscular, or nervous.
-   Explain how tissue structure supports function.
-   Describe structure, function, and location of tissues.
-   Identify epithelial, connective, muscle, and nervous tissues on
    slides.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **Prelab Activity 4.1**

### **Types of Tissues**

-   **Epithelial** – tissue that covers surfaces, lines cavities, and
    forms glands; functions in protection, absorption, filtration, and
    secretion\
-   **Connective** – tissue that supports, binds, and connects other
    tissues\
-   **Muscular** – tissue that contracts to produce movement\
-   **Nervous** – tissue that receives and transmits electrical impulses

------------------------------------------------------------------------

### **Epithelial Tissues**

-   **Simple squamous** – single layer of flat cells for diffusion and
    filtration\
-   **Stratified squamous (keratinized/nonkeratinized)** – multiple
    layers for protection\
-   **Simple cuboidal** – secretion and absorption\
-   **Stratified cuboidal/columnar** – rare protective tissues\
-   **Simple columnar** – absorption and secretion\
-   **Pseudostratified columnar epithelium** – ciliated; mucus movement\
-   **Transitional epithelium** – stretchy epithelium of urinary organs

------------------------------------------------------------------------

### **Epithelial Features**

**Layers** - **Simple** – one layer\
- **Stratified** – multiple layers

**Surfaces** - **Apical** – free surface\
- **Basal** – attached surface\
- **Basement membrane** – anchors epithelium to connective tissue

------------------------------------------------------------------------

### **Connective Tissue**

-   **Cartilage** – flexible support\
-   **Areolar connective tissue** – wraps and cushions\
-   **Hyaline cartilage** – smooth support\
-   **Adipose tissue** – fat storage, insulation\
-   **Fibrocartilage** – tensile strength\
-   **Elastic cartilage** – flexible support\
-   **Dense regular CT** – parallel collagen fibers\
-   **Dense irregular CT** – multidirectional strength\
-   **Bone** – support and mineral storage\
-   **Blood** – transport medium

------------------------------------------------------------------------

### **Connective Tissue Features**

-   **Collagen fibers** – tensile strength\
-   **Elastin fibers** – elasticity\
-   **Extracellular matrix** – support and medium\
-   **Fibroblasts** – fiber production\
-   **Adipocytes** – fat storage\
-   **Chondrocytes** – cartilage cells\
-   **Lacuna** – cavity housing cells

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **Lab Activity 4.1 – Identification Table**

| Image | Name | Location | Function |
|------------------|------------------|------------------|------------------|
| Fig 4.3 | Simple squamous epithelium | Alveoli | Diffusion |
| Fig 4.4 | Simple cuboidal epithelium | Kidney tubules | Secretion & absorption |
| Fig 4.5 | Simple columnar epithelium | Digestive tract | Absorption |
| Fig 4.6 | Stratified squamous epithelium | Epidermis | Protection |
| Fig 4.7 | Pseudostratified columnar epithelium | Trachea | Moves mucus |
| Fig 4.8 | Transitional epithelium | Urinary bladder | Stretching |

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **Lab Activity 4.2 – Microscope Work**

| Tissue              | Magnification | Location        |
|---------------------|---------------|-----------------|
| Simple squamous     | 400x          | Alveoli         |
| Simple cuboidal     | 400x          | Kidney tubules  |
| Simple columnar     | 400x          | Small intestine |
| Transitional        | 400x          | Urinary bladder |
| Stratified squamous | 400x          | Epidermis       |

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **Lab Activity 4.4 – Connective Tissue**

| Tissue            | Magnification | Location             |
|-------------------|---------------|----------------------|
| Areolar           | 400x          | Beneath epithelium   |
| Adipose           | 400x          | Hypodermis           |
| Dense regular CT  | 400x          | Tendons              |
| Blood             | 400x          | Blood vessels        |
| Hyaline cartilage | 400x          | Trachea              |
| Fibrocartilage    | 400x          | Intervertebral discs |
| Elastic cartilage | 400x          | External ear         |
| Bone              | 400x          | Skeleton             |

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **Post Lab Activity 4.1**

-   Epithelium is classified by **layers and cell shape**
-   Major tissues: epithelial, connective, muscle, nervous
-   The **epithelial** tissue contains an apical surface

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **Post Lab Activity 4.2**

1.  Simple squamous epithelium – alveoli\
2.  Simple cuboidal epithelium – kidney tubules\
3.  Stratified squamous epithelium – epidermis\
4.  Areolar connective tissue – beneath epithelium\
5.  Adipose tissue – hypodermis\
6.  Bone – skeletal system

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## **Post Lab Activity 4.3 – Crossword Answers**

**Across** - Simple squamous epithelium - Neuron - Glial cell -
Stratified squamous epithelium - Simple cuboidal epithelium - Skeletal
muscle

**Down** - Dense regular connective tissue - Loose connective tissue -
Bone - Hyaline cartilage - Cardiac muscle - Smooth muscle \`\`
